title: Pascal Triangle
hints: Each element in Pascal's triangle is the sum of the two elements directly above it.
For row i, the first and last elements are always 1.
For any other element at position j in row i, it equals the sum of elements at positions j-1 and j from row i-1.
Initialize a 2D array with zeros, then fill in the values row by row.
The value at position (i, j) is 1 if j == 0 or j == i, otherwise it's the sum of (i-1, j-1) and (i-1, j).
